rulings.Case Title: JITU CURRY ET AL VS. SANDBOX VR, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Complaint; And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2025, Line 3. DEFENDANT SANDBOX VR, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Complaint; And Request For Sanctions. Defendant Sandbox VR's motion to dismiss and request for sanctions is denied in its entirety. Sandbox fails to show that any prejudice it claims to have suffered as a result of the asserted misconduct and neglect of plaintiffs and their counsel could not be remedied by a trial continuance (which, despite plaintiffs' agreement to continue the trial, it never sought) or a discovery motion such as a motion to compel further responses or to compel attendance at a deposition. Nor has Sandbox identified any portion of any order that plaintiffs violated. Nor has Sandbox shown that any of the asserted misconduct and neglect was due to any cause other than the family issues of plaintiffs' counsel and/or a hurricane and thus there is no showing that any misconduct or neglect committed by plaintiffs and their counsel was willful and inexcusable. There is thus no basis to impose the extraordinary sanction of dismissal or even a lesser sanction or penalty of requiring a monetary payment. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: SHAWN SCOTT VS. GROCERY OUTLET INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgement, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2025, Line 4. DEFENDANTs GROCERY OUTLET INC.,and SUB EK INC.'s Motion For Summary Judgement, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendants Grocery Outlet and Sub EK's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied in their entirety. Defendants failed to satisfy their initial motion burden of showing by admissible evidence that plaintiff Shawn Scott cannot prove his negligence and battery claims or his request for punitive damages. Even if defendants did satisfy their burden on any of these matters, paragraphs 4, 6-11 and 13 of the declaration of Mr. Scott shows that there are triable factual disputes as to both of Mr. Scott's claims and his request for punitive damages For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: ETHAN SUTIN VS. X CORP., A NEVADA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend The Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2025, Line 5. PLAINTIFF ETHAN SUTIN's Motion For Leave To Amend The Complaint. Plaintiff Ethan Sutin's motion for leave to amend the complaint is granted conditioned on Mr. Sutin stipulating to a new trial date of November 10, 2025 or any other date mutually agreeable to the parties and joining in an application to request that date from the master calendar department. If Mr. Sutin refuses to accept the condition, the motion is denied. Mr. Sutin may express his acceptance of the condition in a proposed order reflecting the tentative ruling. The correspondence between counsel submitted with the reply papers show that defendant X Corp. believes that its claimed prejudice from the granting of this motion would be eliminated by a trial continuance of 90 days, but that counsel were unable to agree on dates 90 or later than the current trial date due to scheduling conflicts and Mr. Sutin's unwillingness to set a trial date in November. Given the delayed seeking of permission to amend by Mr. Sutin, his refusal to consider a trial date in November is not reasonable. rulings.Case Title: PAUL HENRY VS. ERIC YOUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2025, Line 6. PLAINTIFF PAUL HENRY's Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment. Plaintiff Paul Henry's application for a right to attach order and writ of attachment is denied. Mr. Henry's application is not based on a contract claim. Mr. Henry does not allege that he has a contract with either defendant Eric Young or defendant Memre.AI. Nor are any of the claims in his complaint based on breach of a contract. His first cause of action is for fraud, a tort claim. His second through fifth causes of action are statutory claims. Recognizing that he has not alleged a contract claim in this case, Mr. Henry argues that his claims are "fundamentally contractual" and "rooted" in contractual obligations. But California law requires a contract claim to support an attachment, not a non-contract claim that is fundamentally contractual or rooted in contractual obligations. Mr. Henry does not cite any authority permitting an attachment for a non-contract claim that is fundamentally contractual or rooted in contractual obligations, nor is the court aware of any such authority. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) rulings.Case Title: AMEER ALI ET AL VS. SECURITAS SECURITY SERVICES USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2025, Line 12. DEFENDANT MICHAEL KOPPEL's Amended MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants Securitas Security Services USA and Michael Koppel's motion to strike hearsay statements from the complaint is denied. Paragraphs 19 and 20, including the hearsay statements quoted therein, of the complaint filed by plaintiffs Ameer Ali and Nicholas Kincaid are arguably relevant to their claims for negligent supervision and training. None of the defendants' reasons for striking those paragraphs are persuasive. Contrary to defendants' belief, complaints are rarely read to juries in civil cases, but even if some portions of the complaint is read to the jury, it is unlikely that paragraphs 19 and 20 would be read to the jury. In all events, defendants' appropriate remedy for their concern is to file a motion in limine seeking an order that those paragraphs not be read to the jury. The fact that plaintiffs did not allege more information or context about what they allege in paragraphs 19 and 20 is not grounds to strike those paragraphs. If defendants seek more information from plaintiffs about those paragraphs, they should propound discovery requesting such information. Nor does the court ascribe ill motives to plaintiffs for including paragraphs 19 and 20 in their complaint. Plaintiffs' complaint alleges that defendants committed an array of misconduct against them. Paragraphs 19 and 20, alleging that others experienced similar mistreatment from plaintiffs, are not so far afield from the legal and factual issues raised by the complaint to warrant the striking of those paragraphs or any portions thereof. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: ISELLA CANTU VS. SPRING FERTILITY MANAGEMENT, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2025, Line 9. 1 - DEFENDANTs NAM TRAN, PETER KLATSKY, KIMBERLY KWOK, CHAU DANG, TANYA LY, SPRING FERTILITY MANAGEMENT, LLC, SPRING FERTILITY HOLDINGS, LLC, SPRING FERTILITY MANAGEMENT OAKLAND, LLC, SPRING FERTILITY MANAGEMENT SILICON VALLEY, LLC, and SPRING FERTILITY MASTER MSO, LLC DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to the complaint is off calendar in light of the ruling on their motion to stay. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/HEK)
